{"key_event":"1969 military coup ends parliamentary republic","reasoning":"Independence initially created a sovereign electoral state with active parties, press, clan mobilization, and nationalist politics, but integration problems, patronage, weak fiscal reach, and the chaotic 1969 election eroded institutional legitimacy. By the end of 1969 the SRC coup sharply increased coercive central power while suppressing autonomous parties and constitutional checks, leaving society still moderately organized through clans and politics but less able to constrain the state.","society_change":-0.7,"society_power":5.0,"state_change":0.4,"state_power":6.4}
